text: After visual location of the lesion, we then dissect the fissure and open by using different staplers.
Then we dissect and divide the first pulmonary artery for the upper segment of the lower lobe with a vascular stapler.
After that, we localize a second pulmonary artery for the segment and decide to divide it by using again a vascular stapler.
Just behind the arterial stump we find and dissect the segmental bronchus and for its division we use a medium thick stapler.
We divide the pulmonary ligament in order to allow the remaining lower lobe to expand easily.
and with thick staplers we draw the intersegmental fissure to remove the specimen.
You can see at the end of the image the segmental vein that we divide directly within the fissure.
Here we remove the specimen and as you can see both the upper lobe and the remaining lower lobe expand normally by active insufflation through the facial mask.
As the lower lobe expands normally we detect an air leak in the fissure so we apply glubran sealant all along the fissure.
